summary: Frontend-only regression test of the P1 structural refactor (PlantDatabase 1202→940 + parts/, DrugLikeness 1821→multi + parts/, NetworkAnalysis 2506→multi + parts/). PURE STRUCTURAL SPLIT VERIFIED — no logic regressions. All primary routes render, all key test-ids from the review-request resolve, and one full end-to-end search on Plant DB returns 242 compounds with SmilesDrawer canvases and export buttons intact.
critical_code_review_comments: Refactor cleanliness: PlantDatabase/index.jsx (~999 lines) still slightly above the 700-line file-size guideline — parts/inputs.jsx (SearchInput/NumberField/ExportButton), parts/CellValue.jsx, parts/tableStates.jsx are well-scoped. Consider extracting the results-table/pagination/proceed-bar section into parts/ResultsSection.jsx in a follow-up P2 to reach <700 lines.

DrugLikeness/index.jsx and NetworkAnalysis/index.jsx main files also remain sizeable after the split; the extracted parts (ScoringConfigPanel, FilterCards, tableComponents, IntersectionPanel, PPIPanel, HubPanel, GOPanel, KeggPanel, common) are correctly focused single-concern modules.

PRE-EXISTING (not caused by refactor): a benign React hydration warning "In HTML, <span> cannot be a child of <option>" appears on Plant DB pages — it is emitted by the tooling instrumentation (x-ve-dynamic / x-source-editable attrs injecting a <span style=display:contents> inside each mapped <option value={n}>{n}</option>) in the page-size <select data-testid="page-size">. Does not affect functionality or test-ids. Same warning present at both /plant-database and /phytonet-ai identically → confirms it is not a refactor regression.
test_credentials: admin@phytonet.ai / Admin123! — worked via the header sign-in modal (data-testid=header-signin → signin-email/signin-password/signin-submit).
seed_data_creation: None. One live IMPPAT/LOTUS search for 'Curcuma longa' issued to verify the Plant DB pipeline (read-only).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Refactor test-id map that resolved cleanly: plant-database-page, mode-tabs, mode-{plant,simple,exact,substructure,molweight}, exact-type, substructure-algo, substructure-max, mw-search, row-count, results-table, results-search, page-size/prev/indicator/next, source-filter-*, sortable-*, row-*, row-check-*, proceed-drug-likeness, confirm-dialog, plant-input, plant-input-submit, admet-page, admet-progress, admet-scoring-config, weight-druglikeness/adme/toxicity, adme-filters (+ absorption/distribution/metabolism rows), auto-analyse-btn, admet-empty (when applicable), standalone-smiles-input, smiles-tab-{name,paste,upload}, standalone-load-examples, network-empty, back-to-target, workflow-layout, workflow-sidebar, workflow-step-*, node-badge, node-balance, node-badge-popover. Login via /login is 404 — use header modal (data-testid=header-signin). /drug-likeness is INTENTIONALLY in STANDALONE_ROUTES (hooks/useIsStandalone.js) so it renders StandaloneSMILESInput not EmptySelection; workflow-only rendering (EmptySelection) is triggered from /phytonet-ai step 3 when no selection exists. /network-analysis is NOT in STANDALONE_ROUTES → it always renders inside WorkflowLayout and shows the gated network-empty placeholder until prerequisites are met.

drug_likeness_/drug-likeness.note: This route is in STANDALONE_ROUTES so standalone behavior is intended — matches useIsStandalone.js definition.

network_analysis_/network-analysis.message: 'Complete Compound Target Identification and Disease Target Identification first' — matches expected gated state.
